ordering_variable: morphological stage T / bulge-to-disk / early-to-late sequence
project_relevance: Classical morphology orders stellar concentration, gas fraction, color, and star-formation tendency, but it is not a one-dimensional physical clock.
equation_hint: Use T only as a weak prior or side-channel: late-type/gas-rich systems should not automatically be treated as dynamically mature.
claim_boundary: Morphology alone cannot determine activation; SPARC is disk-biased and T is coarse.

canonical_sources: Dressler 1980; Kauffmann et al. 2004; Blanton & Moustakas 2009
project_relevance: Environment changes morphology and quenching pathway; isolated gas-rich dwarfs and disturbed/tidal objects must be separated.
equation_hint: Add an environment/disturbance quarantine multiplier rather than folding every outlier into the same scalar maturity axis.
claim_boundary: Environment columns are not available in the current SPARC table; use as follow-up flag only.

canonical_sources: Noeske et al. 2007; Speagle et al. 2014; Brinchmann et al. 2004
ordering_variable: SFR-Mstar offset / sSFR / star-forming vs quenched state
project_relevance: Galaxy evolution often tracks distance from the star-forming main sequence; this is closer to gas-processing state than morphology alone.
equation_hint: If SFR exists, add a gas-processing/depletion coordinate: sSFR or SFE should govern transition speed, not just current f_gas.
claim_boundary: SFR is missing from the current SPARC-lite inputs; use xGASS/xCOLD/MaNGA proxy layer until direct SPARC SFR is added.

canonical_sources: Lilly et al. 2013; Dekel & Mandelker 2014; Tacchella et al. 2016
ordering_variable: gas inflow/outflow equilibrium; depletion time; gas fraction
project_relevance: Gas-regulator models treat galaxies as reservoirs whose state depends on inflow, star formation, outflow, and depletion time.
equation_hint: Replace pure product gate with a reservoir disequilibrium term: activation rises when gas conversion and surface-density compaction outrun H I reservoir extension.
claim_boundary: Current table has f_gas but not inflow/outflow/SFR; a diagnostic proxy is possible, accepted law is not.

canonical_sources: Kennicutt 1998; Martin & Kennicutt 2001; Toomre 1964; Romeo & Mogotsi 2017
ordering_variable: gas/stellar surface density and disk stability Q
project_relevance: Star formation and disk settling depend on surface density and stability threshold; low surface-density gas-rich systems remain weakly coupled.
equation_hint: Keep Sigma_b but use it with compaction/stability form, e.g. Sigma_b times stellar conversion over H I extension; do not let high gas alone activate.
claim_boundary: Toomre Q needs velocity dispersion and epicyclic frequency; not available in SPARC scalar table.

canonical_sources: Fall & Efstathiou 1980; Fall & Romanowsky 2013; Obreschkow & Glazebrook 2014
ordering_variable: specific angular momentum j and spin-like ordering
project_relevance: Morphology and disk/spheroid state are strongly organized by specific angular momentum at fixed mass.
equation_hint: Promising alternative: use a nonleaky angular-momentum residual or spin proxy as a separate order parameter: settled disks have coherent j; disturbed/underrotating systems do not.
claim_boundary: Observed j uses V and can leak into A_inferred; use nonleaky proxy or out-of-fold residual with strict tests.

canonical_sources: Cappellari et al. 2011; Emsellem et al. 2011; Krajnovic et al. 2011
project_relevance: Ellipticals/lenticulars are better ordered by kinematic angular momentum than visual morphology.
equation_hint: For external early-type bridge, activation should be a rotation-support coordinate, not a Hubble-type coordinate.
claim_boundary: lambda_R not available for SPARC; proxy layer only.

canonical_sources: McGaugh 2005; McGaugh 2012; Lelli et al. 2016; McGaugh, Lelli & Schombert 2016
project_relevance: These are endpoint dynamical relations. They are close to the target rather than independent maturity predictors.
equation_hint: Use BTFR/RAR only for validation/residual definition, not base ordering features, unless explicitly labeled leaky upper-bound diagnostic.
claim_boundary: alpha_BTFR or RAR-derived variables reuse velocity information and can inflate rho.

canonical_sources: Broeils & Rhee 1997; Wang et al. 2016; Lelli et al. 2016
claim_boundary: H I extent tracks whether gas is diffuse reservoir or compact participating disk.
column7: Refine A_HI: compare observed R_HI/R_d against mass-dependent expected H I size, not a fixed threshold alone.
column8: Requires H I size/mass calibration; current RHI/Rd threshold is crude.

canonical_sources: Kassin et al. 2012; Simons et al. 2017; TNG50 disk evolution papers
project_relevance: Disks settle through declining turbulence and increasing ordered rotation, not just through increasing stellar fraction.
equation_hint: Add a settling coordinate where activation depends on ordered support/coherence. In SPARC scalar data this can only be approximated by j/spin or residual structure.
claim_boundary: Need velocity dispersion/2D kinematics for real test.

canonical_sources: Dekel & Burkert 2014; Tacchella et al. 2016; Zolotov et al. 2015
project_relevance: Evolution can be non-monotonic: gas-rich compaction can raise central density before quenching or spheroid formation.
equation_hint: Use two axes rather than one: reservoir maturity and compaction/central-density maturity. Product gate is too brittle if these axes evolve differently.
claim_boundary: Current SPARC table has disk-average Sigma_b, not central density.

canonical_sources: Toomre 1977; Barnes & Hernquist 1992; Hopkins et al. 2009
ordering_variable: merger-driven spheroid formation and angular-momentum loss
project_relevance: Spiral/elliptical formation includes multiple channels, not a single spiral-to-elliptical ladder.
equation_hint: Introduce branch labels: disk-settling sequence vs disturbed/merger/spheroid branch. Do not force all objects onto one scalar clock.
claim_boundary: SPARC disk sample cannot directly calibrate spheroid branch.

column9: Modern simulations/surveys use multi-axis state spaces rather than one morphology sequence.
column10: Best proposed scheme: two-axis base equation with reservoir conversion R and dynamical settling J, plus quarantine/environment branch flag.
column11: Simulation consistency is conceptual until matched variables are ingested.
